description_long: This object is a collection of various metallic hardware components, likely door handles and levers, arranged in a row. The pieces are primarily composed of metal, with some parts featuring a dark, possibly rubberized or painted finish, and one component displaying a wood-like texture. The handles exhibit different shapes, including straight, curved, and angular forms, and some are attached to mounting plates or escutcheons. The metallic surfaces are mostly smooth and reflective, with a few matte or textured areas. The set includes both modern and traditional handle designs, suggesting a display or assortment for selection. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a focus on function and durability.
description: A set of assorted metallic door handles and levers, featuring various shapes and finishes, including metal and wood-like textures. The components are arranged in a row, showcasing both modern and traditional designs.
description_view_0: A frontal view showing the full assortment of handles and levers, highlighting their different shapes, finishes, and mounting plates.
description_view_1: A side view revealing the thickness and profile of each handle, as well as the mounting hardware and attachment points.
description_view_2: A rear view displaying the backs of the handles and levers, with less detail on the mounting plates and more focus on the handle shapes.
description_view_3: An opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the profiles and some internal mounting structures of the handles.
attribute_storefront_layout: The exterior consists of a linear arrangement of various door handles and levers, each displayed separately with visible mounting hardware. There are no visible entrances, windows, or display areas beyond the hardware items themselves.
